title: Neonauclea morindifolia
intro: Neonauclea morindifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neonauclea morindifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neonauclea morindifolia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make a tea or powder, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
